title: Massaman Curry

overview.description: A rich Southern Thai beef curry with potatoes, peanuts and homemade Massaman curry paste. Slow cooked until the beef is meltingly tender.

ingredients: 800g beef shin, cut into large chunks
2 medium potatoes, cut into large chunks
method: Heat the oil over a medium-high heat.
Pour in half the coconut milk and simmer until slightly thickened and the oil begins to separate.
Return the beef to the pan.
Add the remaining coconut milk and the beef stock.
Stir in the fish sauce, tamarind paste and palm sugar.
Cover and simmer gently for 1½ hours.
Add the potatoes, onion and roasted peanuts.
Continue cooking for a further 45–60 minutes until the beef is fork tender and the potatoes are cooked.

learning.did-you-know: Massaman curry is one of Thailand's most internationally celebrated dishes and reflects centuries of influence from Persian, Malay and Indian traders.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a slow-cooked Thai curry, cook with homemade curry paste and balance sweet, savoury and tangy flavours.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A comforting slow-cooked curry that makes excellent use of homemade Massaman Curry Paste while using inexpensive cuts of beef.
chapman-tips: One frozen portion of Massaman Curry Paste is all you need for this recipe.
Beef shin gives the richest flavour after a long, gentle cook.
Roasted peanuts add both texture and authentic flavour.
This curry is even better the following day.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently on the hob. The flavour develops beautifully overnight.

similar-recipes: Gaeng Hang Lay (Northern Thai Pork Curry)
